title: Calendula oat soap
subtitle: An olive and shea cold-process bar with calendula-infused oil
excerpt: A cold-process bar built on olive oil infused with dried calendula petals, shea butter for extra conditioning, and finely milled oat at trace. The calendula infusion turns the oil a warm gold; the bar after cure is mild, slightly exfoliating, and well-suited to dry skin.
sourceNotes: Susan Miller Cavitch, The Soapmaker's Companion (1997), Ch. 4, soft-oil blends and cure time. Kevin Dunn, Caveman Chemistry (2003), Ch. 9, fatty-acid profile of olive and shea. SoapCalc SAP-value tables (v2024): olive 0.135, shea 0.128, coconut 0.190, castor 0.128.

content.text: A cold-process calendula oat soap. The olive oil base is infused with dried calendula petals 2-4 weeks ahead; the infusion turns the oil a warm gold and draws some of the petals' skin-soothing compounds into the oil phase. Shea butter at 10% adds a conditioning quality to the superfat. Finely milled oat folded in at trace gives a gentle physical exfoliation.
The lye calculation at 5%
superfat
What you need

items.name: Olive oil
items.prepNote: pre-infused with dried calendula petals (see method). 50% of the oil weight.

items.name: Coconut oil
items.prepNote: 76°F-melt-point. 20% of the oil weight. Provides lather.

items.name: Shea butter
items.prepNote: unrefined shea butter. 20% of the oil weight. Conditioning superfat.

items.name: Castor oil (cosmetic)
items.prepNote: cold-pressed castor oil. 10% of the oil weight. Stabilises lather.

items.name: Distilled water
items.prepNote: distilled or deionised. 33% of the oil weight.

items.name: Sodium hydroxide (NaOH)
items.prepNote: 99% pure cosmetic-grade NaOH at 5% superfat. Weigh to 0.1 g precision.

items.name: Colloidal oatmeal
items.prepNote: finely milled oat for gentle exfoliation. Added at light trace. 2% of the oil weight.

content.text: Method
Prepare the calendula infusion (2-4 weeks ahead)

Fill a clean jar with 500 g of refined olive oil and add 2 tablespoons of dried calendula petals. Seal the jar and leave in a cool, dark cupboard for 2-4 weeks, shaking every few days. The oil will deepen from pale yellow to warm gold. Strain out the petals through a fine-mesh sieve lined with muslin before using in the soap batch. The infused oil has the same SAP value as plain olive oil for lye calculation purposes.
Mix the lye solution

Weigh 330 g of distilled water into the HDPE pitcher. Add 137 g NaOH into the water in a steady stream, stirring continuously. The pitcher heats immediately and fumes briefly; do not lean over it. Stir until clear, then set aside to cool to 38-42°C.
Melt and warm the oils

Weigh the calendula-infused olive oil, coconut oil, shea butter, and castor oil into a saucepan. The coconut oil and shea butter are solid; warm on a low heat until both have melted and the mixture is clear and homogenous. Remove from heat and check the temperature; the oil phase needs to be in the 38-42°C window.
Combine and blend to light trace
When both phases are at 38-42°C, pour the lye solution into the oils. Pulse the stick blender in 10-second bursts until the batter thickens to
light trace
. The shea butter can cause the batter to accelerate trace faster than a pure olive-oil recipe; stop blending the moment you see the custard-consistency thickening.
Fold in the oat and pour

At light trace, scatter the 20 g of finely milled oat over the surface and fold through with the silicone spatula in 30-second strokes until evenly distributed. Pour into the silicone mould, tap to settle, and smooth the surface. Cover with a tea towel and leave undisturbed for 24 hours.
Cure: 4-6 weeks
Unmould after 24 hours and cut into bars. Stand on a wire rack.
Cure
for 4-6 weeks with the bars turned at 2 weeks. The bars will be a soft gold from the calendula infusion; the colour does not fade significantly during cure.
Patch test

Patch-test a sliver on the inside of the forearm for 24 to 48 hours. Calendula is a gentle herb. It belongs to the daisy family. If you have reacted to ragweed, chrysanthemum, or chamomile before, use the bar with care.
Storage and shelf life

Two years in a cool dark cupboard once fully cured. The calendula infusion introduces slightly more unsaturated fatty acids than plain olive, making the bar marginally more susceptible to DOS. A consistently cool storage location prevents this. Discard any bar that develops orange flecks or a sharp paint-like smell.
